fact: The first official Test cricket match was played between Australia and England in 1877 at the Melbourne Cricket Ground.
India won its first Cricket World Cup in 1983 under the captaincy of Kapil Dev.
The ICC Men's Cricket World Cup is held every four years.
Sachin Tendulkar is the highest run scorer in international cricket.
Virat Kohli has scored over 50 international centuries across formats.
The first FIFA World Cup was held in Uruguay in 1930.
Brazil has won the FIFA World Cup five times, more than any other nation.
The UEFA Champions League is Europe's premier club football competition.
A standard football match lasts 90 minutes excluding extra time.
Lionel Messi won the FIFA World Cup with Argentina in 2022.
The Thomas Cup is the world championship for men's badminton teams.
India won its first Thomas Cup title in 2022.
P. V. Sindhu won a silver medal at the 2016 Olympics.
Saina Nehwal won India's first Olympic badminton medal in 2012.
A badminton match is played as the best of three games to 21 points.
There are four Grand Slam tournaments: Australian Open, French Open, Wimbledon and US Open.
Wimbledon is the oldest tennis tournament in the world.
Roger Federer has won eight Wimbledon men's singles titles.
Rafael Nadal is known as the King of Clay.
Novak Djokovic has won multiple Grand Slam championships across all surfaces.
The NBA was founded in 1946.
A basketball team has five players on the court.
Michael Jordan won six NBA championships with the Chicago Bulls.
The three-point line awards three points for successful long-range shots.
LeBron James is one of the highest-scoring players in NBA history.
